page: Vultr vbm-64c-1536gb-amd is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal NVMe family. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC 9354 processor featuring 64 physical cores and 128 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is provisioned with a massive memory capacity of 1536.0 GB, yielding a high resource density of 12.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

For local storage, the configuration includes a 480 GB NVMe SSD, providing fast read and write speeds for operating system files and critical application data. The server does not include any dedicated graphics processing units (GPUs). Network connectivity is supported by one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 12:1, this bare metal instance is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring large in-memory datasets without the overhead of virtualization. It is well-suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and heavy data processing pipelines.
description: Vultr vbm-64c-1536gb-amd is a high-capacity bare metal server from the Bare Metal NVMe family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9354 processor. It features 64 physical cores and 128 vCPUs running at 3.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. With an extensive memory allocation of 1536.0 GB, the server provides 12.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, making it highly optimized for memory-intensive applications. Local storage is provided by a 480 GB NVMe SSD, and the system includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. This server is designed for demanding workloads such as large-scale databases, in-memory data stores, and intensive enterprise data processing.
